title: Core: Fix WebSocket connection for StackBlitz/WebContainers
body:
Closes #34226

<!-- If your PR is related to an issue, provide the number(s) above; if it resolves multiple issues, be sure to break them up (e.g. "closes #1000, closes #1001"). -->

<!--

Thank you for contributing to Storybook! Please submit all PRs to the `next` branch unless they are specific to the current release. Storybook maintainers cherry-pick bug and documentation fixes into the `main` branch as part of the release process, so you shouldn't need to worry about this. For additional guidance: https://storybook.js.org/docs/contribute

-->

## What I did

- Detect WebContainer environment using `@webcontainer/env`
- Disable origin/host and websocket token validation when running in a WebContainer.

## Checklist for Contributors

### Testing

<!-- Please check (put an "x" inside the "[ ]") the applicable items below to communicate how to test your changes -->

#### The changes in this PR are covered in the following automated tests:

- [ ] stories
- [x] unit tests
- [ ] integration tests
- [ ] end-to-end tests

#### Manual testing

- Take the StackBlitz sandbox from #34226. Confirm it throws a websocket token validation error in console, and the UI notifies that the server is disconnected.
- Upgrade the sandbox to the canary version below.
- Restart the Storybook and confirm the websocket validation error no longer occurs, and the notification doesn't appear.

### Documentation

<!-- Please check (put an "x" inside the "[ ]") the applicable items below to indicate which documentation has been updated. -->

- [ ] Add or update documentation reflecting your changes
- [ ] If you are deprecating/removing a feature, make sure to update
      [MIGRATION.MD](https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/blob/next/MIGRATION.md)

## Checklist for Maintainers

- [x] When this PR is ready for testing, make sure to add `ci:normal`, `ci:merged` or `ci:daily` GH label to it to run a specific set of sandboxes. The particular set of sandboxes can be found in `code/lib/cli-storybook/src/sandbox-templates.ts`
- [x] Make sure this PR contains **one** of the labels below:
   <details>
     <summary>Available labels</summary>

  - `bug`: Internal changes that fixes incorrect behavior.
  - `maintenance`: User-facing maintenance tasks.
  - `dependencies`: Upgrading (sometimes downgrading) dependencies.
  - `build`: Internal-facing build tooling & test updates. Will not show up in release changelog.
  - `cleanup`: Minor cleanup style change. Will not show up in release changelog.
  - `documentation`: Documentation **only** changes. Will not show up in release changelog.
  - `feature request`: Introducing a new feature.
  - `BREAKING CHANGE`: Changes that break compatibility in some way with current major version.
  - `other`: Changes that don't fit in the above categories.

   </details>

author: ghengeveld

comments.author: ghengeveld
comments.body: Not sure whether a re-export is preferable. Seemed like a good idea because we also have `isCI` in this file.

comments.author: AriPerkkio
comments.body:
This feels quite scary to be honest. We disable all authentication checks based on value a third party package returns. A bug or compromise in that package could lead to wide attack vector.

Do you think this is valid concern?

I'd be much more confident to check only `process.versions.webcontainer`. 🤔

comments.author: AriPerkkio
comments.body: Though same issue would happen if any other package populated that `process.versions` field on runtime. 🤷

comments.author: ghengeveld
comments.body:
Yeah, I contemplated this but dismissed it as "if that happens, you're already compromised anyway". And indeed, any package could populate that.

We could make this behavior configurable. Either allow people to opt-out (increasing security but otherwise not gaining anything), or require opt-in (in which case we need to update our sandboxes to set this opt-in). The latter would make it harder to use non-sandbox Storybooks on StackBlitz though.

comments.author: ghengeveld
comments.body: Discussed with @shilman, and we're accepting the risk.
